name: Push Fix to GitHub & Create PR
description: When the Kiro agent finishes a task that includes a code fix, this hook automatically creates a branch, commits the changes, pushes to GitHub, and opens a pull request using the GitHub MCP.

then.prompt:
The agent has just completed a task. Check if any source files in `src/` or `tests/` were modified during this session.

If files WERE modified, do the following using the GitHub MCP:

1. Determine the Jira ticket ID from the recent conversation context (e.g., DEMO-1). If none found, use 'fix/auto-patch'.
2. Create a new branch named: `fix/<TICKET-ID>-<short-kebab-description>` (e.g., fix/DEMO-1-division-by-zero).
3. Commit all changed files to that branch. Use this commit message format:
   `fix(<scope>): <what was fixed> — resolves <TICKET-ID>`
   Example: `fix(calculator): throw error on division by zero — resolves DEMO-1`
4. Push the branch to the remote repository using `push_files`.
5. Create a pull request with:
   - Title: same as commit message
   - Body: include the Jira ticket ID, what was changed, and what tests now pass
   - Base branch: main
6. Report the PR URL to the user.

If NO files were modified, do nothing and stay silent.
